cards.back: A model where 'p' is the number of autoregressive terms, 'd' is the degree of differencing, and 'q' is the number of moving average terms.
The part of the ARIMA model that captures the relationship between current and past values of the time series data.
The part of the ARIMA model that captures the relationship between current and past errors or residuals in the time series data.
The process of subtracting a constant from each value in a time series to make it stationary, used to remove non-stationarity in the data.
The property of a time series that implies that its statistical parameters (mean, variance, autocorrelation) remain constant over time, which is essential for ARIMA modeling.
